next_step_text: Hi Reema, Colin, Attila, Michelle, Shrikant, and team, I hope you are all having a great week. I wanted to check back in following our initial RFI discussions and demonstrations. I know your team was planning to evaluate the full field of vendors to see how Zycus fits into your broader procurement strategy. Now that a bit of time has passed, I would love to reconnect briefly to get your feedback a

our_open_deliverables.text: Forward RFI response to Reema Gupta and coordinate demo timing with Shrikant
Provide a CDR5 pre-demo discovery document before finalizing the demo date
Respond to RFI including NDA and detailed system/integration information
Deliver customized demo covering sourcing, procurement, supplier onboarding, and CLM (with focus on supplier onboarding automation and AI CLM redlining)
Follow up next week (week of 2026-06-30) to discuss RFI feedback, commercials, pricing sensitivities, ROI, and any deeper-dive areas (AP, supplier onboarding, CLM)
Share demo slide deck and coordinate customer references if helpful

buyer_dependent_items.text: Share RFI response feedback, competitive scoring vs other shortlisted vendors, and shortlist decision timeline
Continue with other vendor demos as part of RFI process and let Zycus know how Zycus fits in once buyer has seen the full shortlist field

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the named incumbent being displaced on cost-vs-value grounds; the strongest current threat. Buyer replacing Coupa because 'too expensive relative to value they provide' (Shrikant 2026-06-11), but Coupa remains a threat if it drops price to retain the account. Other shortlisted vendors unknown (buyer disclosed 'other vendors we have shortlisted' but did not name them, Reema 2026-06-02); competitive landscape beyond Coupa is a black box, preventing tailored differentiation. Zycus entered late ('last minute,' Attila 2026-06-02) and must catch up. Without knowing the other shortlisted vendors or Coupa's retention strategy, Zycus cannot position against the full competitive field. Immediate risk: a lower-cost challenger or Coupa price concession wins if Zycus does not anchor pricing to quantified savings and recruit a champion before shortlist decision (imminent, early July 2026).

competitors.name: SAP Coupa
competitors.quote: The current solutions, Coupa and, are too expensive relative to the value they provide.
competitors.source: Avoma demo transcript, Shrikant Rajupalepu, ~7176-second mark
competitors.how_we_win: Coupa is being replaced on cost. Zycus wins by quantifying ROI (supplier onboarding cycle time reduction, integration error elimination, system consolidation savings) and pricing below Coupa while demonstrating superior supplier onboarding automation (Attila's top pain) and AI CLM redlining (Akumark's stated need). Risk: Coupa may drop price to retain; without a champion and quantified ROI, Avaya may accept a Coupa discount.

competitors.name: Unknown shortlisted vendors (multiple)
competitors.quote: We do plan to issue an RFI to other vendors as well that we have shortlisted. Zycus came into picture kind of last minute; we already started this process and you have a lot of catch-up to do.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call, Reema Gupta and Attila Abraham
competitors.how_we_win: Other vendors are unknown but were invited earlier and have a head start. Zycus wins by: (1) debriefing RFI results with Reema to learn the competitive field and how Zycus scored, (2) recruiting a champion (likely Attila) who will sell Zycus internally and provide competitive intel, (3) engaging CIO and Akumark (final decision-makers) directly before shortlist decision to validate strategic fit and budget, and (4) quantifying ROI to justify pricing vs lower-cost challengers. Immediate move: ask Reema on next call (week of 2026-06-30) for shortlist feedback and competitive positioning.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: The current solutions, Cooper and Rebug, are deemed too expensive relative to the value they provide.
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor a quantified savings-vs-current-spend TCO model and lead on integrated single-platform value plus supplier-onboarding automation.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Ariba was there, but we still use Ariba as more a customer, not as a vendor.
competitors.how_we_win: Not an active replacement target; position Zycus as the consolidated modern S2P that retires the legacy footprint.

competitors.name: Unnamed shortlisted vendors
competitors.quote: we do plan to issue an RFI... to other vendors as well that we have shortlisted, and then from that RFI, we will be shortlisting the vendors
competitors.how_we_win: Get the RFI scoring and the rival shortlist from Attila/Reema so differentiation and pricing can be tailored before the cut.

competitors.name: Coupa (SAP)
competitors.quote: Coupa/SAP too expensive relative to the value they provide
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor Zycus pricing to quantified TCO savings vs. Coupa; demonstrate single-platform integration advantage (eliminate swivel-chair integration and point-of-failure risk Coupa creates); lead with Merlin Intake, AI sourcing, and supplier onboarding automation (Attila's top pain) as embedded AI workflow differentiators. Recruit Attila as champion to sell these internally before shortlist decision.

competitors.name: Unknown shortlisted vendors
competitors.quote: We plan to issue RFI to Zycus and other vendors we have shortlisted
competitors.how_we_win: Discover who else is shortlisted (ask Reema/Attila directly or via champion development); once known, tailor differentiation to their weaknesses. Without knowing the field, default to Zycus's core differentiators: full S2P single-platform, embedded AI (not bolt-on), supplier onboarding automation, and quantified ROI vs. incumbent Coupa cost.

champion_strength.summary: No champion yet; best candidate is Attila Abraham (Purchasing Manager EMEA, GSO operations) but he is developing and has not yet acted as advocate. Attila owns the top pain (supplier onboarding automation), participated actively in discovery (2026-06-02) and demo (2026-06-11), disclosed that Zycus entered late ('came into picture kind of last minute') and urged Zycus to accelerate ('any help on that front to see it Monday Tuesday would appreciate it'). However, no evidence yet of advocacy: no coaching on competitive threats, no CIO/Akumark introduction, no internal selling for Zycus vs. other shortlisted vendors. Recruiting Attila as champion before shortlist decision (imminent, early July 2026) is the highest-leverage near-term move. Without a champion, Zycus is at risk of being out-sold by a competitor who recruits an internal advocate first.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-02, Shrikant: 'final sign-off will be CIO, Ramesh hierarchy leadership, Reema and Akumark leadership'
Avoma call 2026-06-02, Reema: 'Internally, we have a number in place, budget allocated'
Salesforce Contact: John Hill, CTO (labeled CIO on contact list, not confirmed on calls)
Salesforce Contact: Akumark (referenced on calls, full name/title not disclosed)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Economic buyer role identified but not yet engaged. Shrikant disclosed (2026-06-02) that final sign-off requires 'CIO as well as Ramesh hierarchy leadership, and Reema and Akumark's leadership.' Per Salesforce contact list, CIO is John Hill (not verified on calls). Akumark (full name/title not disclosed on calls) is GSO/procurement leadership. Reema Gupta stated (2026-06-02) 'Internally, we have a number in place... we have the budget allocated,' confirming budget authority exists and deal is funded. However, neither CIO nor Akumark has appeared on any call or activity to date. Zycus is engaging mid-level evaluators (Reema, Shrikant, Attila) who will recommend but do not control the final decision. Without direct CIO/Akumark engagement, Zycus cannot validate strategic fit, budget envelope, or decision criteria at the level where the shortlist and vendor-selection decisions are made. Risk: if CIO or Akumark favor incumbent or another vendor, mid-level advocates may not have influence to override.

recommended_moves: Recruit Attila Abraham as champion by delivering supplier onboarding ROI model (quantify cycle-time and FTE savings) and coaching him to sell Zycus internally vs other shortlisted vendors before shortlist decision.

Deliver quantified ROI and TCO model vs Coupa (cycle-time reduction, FTE savings, error-rate improvement, platform consolidation savings) to Reema and Attila before commercials discussion (week of 2026-06-30).
Request Reema or Attila to introduce Edward to the relevant stakeholder and Akumark for strategic fit validation and decision-criteria alignment before shortlist decision (early July 2026).
